items.title: NHL commissioner says criticism of U.S. men's hockey team at Olympics 'unfortunate'
items.summary_auto: NHL commissioner Gary Bettman declined an opportunity Tuesday to weigh in on the political aspect of the U.S. hockey celebration at the Milan-Cortina Olympics but did say he thought what was portrayed "was unfortunate and not accurate."

items.title: Bettman says criticism of U.S. men's hockey team at Olympics 'unfortunate'
items.summary_auto: NHL commissioner Gary Bettman declined an opportunity Tuesday to weigh in on the political aspect of the U.S. hockey celebration at the Milan-Cortina Olympics but did say he thought what was portrayed "was unfortunate and not accurate."

items.title: Convicted fraudster who admitted he 'destroyed families' pleads guilty mid-trial
items.summary_auto: A few years after he was released from prison after serving more than five years for fraud, Scott Brooks began targeting victims once again, a judge heard Tuesday as Brooks pleaded guilty mid-trial to six counts of fraud.

items.title: Israel sends troops to southern Lebanon, as Hezbollah vows it's ready for 'open war'
items.summary_auto: Israel sent troops into southern Lebanon on Tuesday and warned residents of more than 80 villages to evacuate as the Iran-backed militant Hezbollah group said it was ready for an "open war" with Israel in the wake of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

items.title: How Iran is strangling the world's oil supply | About That
items.summary_auto: Iran effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route, threatening to attack any ship trying to pass through. Andrew Chang explains why the country is able to have such an impact, and to what extent the ripple effects may be felt. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: Live Nation's antitrust trial is underway in the U.S. What could it mean for Canada?
items.summary_auto: The antitrust trial between Ticketmaster’s parent company and the U.S. Department of Justice got underway with opening statements on Tuesday. The case could force a breakup of Ticketmaster and Live Nation and have ripple effects in Canada — but experts say it’s still early, and bets on the outcome are off the table.

items.title: Live Nation's antitrust trial is underway in the U.S. What's at stake in the case?
items.summary_auto: The antitrust trial between Ticketmaster’s parent company and the U.S. Department of Justice got underway with opening statements on Tuesday. The case could force a breakup of Ticketmaster and Live Nation and have ripple effects in Canada — but experts say it’s still early, and bets on the outcome are off the table.

items.title: Fans will 'soon' learn if Calgary-Edmonton bid to host 2028 World Cup of Hockey a success
items.summary_auto: Speaking in Calgary on Tuesday, NHL commissioner Gary Bettman says a decision will be made "soon" on who will host the 2028 World Cup of Hockey.

items.title: 3 children dead in winter activity incidents are a reminder of the dangers involved
items.summary_auto: Since early February, at least three children have died in winter-related activities in Quebec alone. The Montreal Children’s Hospital’s trauma director says this is a reminder that activities like sledding should be undertaken with caution and parental supervision.

items.title: Canadians in Persian Gulf say they are living by the grace of missile defence systems
items.summary_auto: Canadian expats and those trying to get back home from the Persian Gulf say they're living under the constant threat of missile and drone strikes, but are taking solace in what they describe as highly effective air-defence systems keeping them safe from harm.

items.title: She was about to take her Ottawa business national, when the Spice Girls' lawyers threatened to sue
items.summary_auto: An Ottawa woman who has been selling her custom spice blends since she was 13 says she's being forced to change her company's name after lawyers for the pop band Spice Girls threatened to sue her.

items.title: Carney says his support for U.S. and Israel's war on Iran 'not a blank cheque'
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney says his support for U.S President Donald Trump's attack on Iran comes amid a "failure of the international order" and is "not a blank cheque."

items.title: 6 Toronto police officers charged in corruption probe were supposed to appear in court. Why didn’t they?
items.summary_auto: Their absences aren’t actually against the law. But because this is a high-profile case and none of the officers scheduled to appear Tuesday are in custody, questions persist about when they’ll finally make a public appearance.

items.title: Class action lawsuit over migrant farm workers' EI benefits, restrictions to proceed
items.summary_auto: A class action lawsuit alleging Canada's Seasonal Agricultural Worker Program exploits the labour and Charter rights of agricultural workers can proceed, a judge decided last week.

items.title: How Western countries are handling getting citizens out of the Middle East
items.summary_auto: As the effects of the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran spread across the Middle East, major Western countries are scrambling to make arrangements for getting their citizens out of the region.

items.title: After fatal collisions, Northern Ontario NDP MPPs raise highway safety concerns through road trip
items.summary_auto: NDP members of provincial parliament are embarking on a nine-day road trip across northern Ontario to raise awareness about ongoing safety concerns along the Highway 11-17 corridor. Highway safety has been top of mind in the region following a recent string of fatal collisions, where eight deaths were reported in a 10-day period. Here's what we know about the tour, called “Our Roads, Our Safety.”

items.title: Queen's University group stranded in Middle East looking for a way home
items.summary_auto: Sixteen students and their professor from Queen's University are safe in Doha, Qatar, after their plan was turned around mid-air on Feb. 28.

items.title: Poilievre touts 'free trade' between 'free nations' in speech on first foreign trip
Kristi Noem stands by remarks accusing U.S. citizens killed in Minneapolis of terrorism
items.summary_auto: U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem is standing by her remarks calling the acts of two U.S. citizens shot dead by federal immigration officers in Minneapolis "domestic terrorism."

items.title: Iran holds funerals for girls, staff killed in air strike on school
items.summary_auto: Thousands of people gathered in Minab, Iran, for mass funerals of victims killed on Saturday when a girl’s elementary school was hit by airstrikes. Iranian media is reporting that 168 children were killed and the UN's High Commissioner for Human Rights is calling for an ‘impartial and thorough’ investigation into the strike.

items.title: Sharp rise in young men contacting Ontario gambling helpline, researchers find
items.summary_auto: After online gambling was privatized in Ontario, contacts to Ontario’s mental health helpline for gambling-related problems increased 317 per cent in men and adolescent boys aged 15 to 24, a new study suggests.

items.title: 6 takeaways from Bill Clinton's deposition about Epstein and Maxwell
items.summary_auto: In over four hours of testimony, former U.S. president Bill Clinton denied any knowledge of criminal activities by convicted sex offenders Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell, and said he doubted that Epstein was working for an intelligence service when he knew the late financier.

items.title: Charting Iran's complex regime: Who's dead and who remains
items.summary_auto: Following joint U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran last weekend, several key members of the Iranian regime are reportedly dead. Here is a detailed look at the complexity and depth of Iran's regime.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | See the 'blood moon' eclipse
items.summary_auto: Moongazers across Canada and abroad got treated to a full lunar eclipse early Tuesday.

items.title: B.C. chief coroner announces inquest into Tumbler Ridge tragedy
items.summary_auto: B.C. 's chief coroner has announced an inquest into the last month's mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge.

items.title: Alberta drone company gets $1.1M federal loan for weather and combat testing
items.summary_auto: Landing Zones Canada, based in Medicine Hat, Alta., is receiving a $1.1-million loan from Prairies Economic Development Canada to help further develop and bring drones to market.

items.title: Justin Timberlake doesn't want impaired-driving arrest footage released. He's suing to stop it
items.summary_auto: Justin Timberlake is suing to block the release of police body camera footage from his impaired-driving arrest in New York's Hamptons in 2024.

items.title: Why the Chinese Communist Party's largest annual gathering matters
items.summary_auto: As China's biggest political meetings of the year kick off on Wednesday, the world's second-largest economy will deliver policy announcements and economic targets amid U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs and a contracting domestic economy.

items.title: 'Absolutely not': Complainant denies having consensual sex with Stronach in sex assault trial
items.summary_auto: The seventh complainant in the sexual assault trial of Frank Stronach engaged in consensual sexual acts with the Canadian businessman, his lawyer charged on Tuesday.

items.title: Wall Street, global markets partly recover after early sell-off sparked by war with Iran
items.summary_auto: A sell-off for stocks wrapped around the world and hit Wall Street Tuesday, though the losses eased significantly as the day progressed. Oil prices, meanwhile, leaped even higher on worries about the widening war with Iran.

items.title: Prediction market bets on Iran strikes spur allegations of insider trading
items.summary_auto: Bets placed on the ouster of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei drew scrutiny of prediction markets such as Polymarket and Kalshi on Monday, sparking calls from Democratic U.S. lawmakers to outlaw wagers on military actions ‌that could enrich officials with inside knowledge.

items.title: N.L. reaches deal to open path for construction and production of Bay du Nord offshore oil project
items.summary_auto: Newfoundland and Labrador’s provincial government has signed an agreement with Equinor and BP to open the path for construction and production of the Bay du Nord offshore oil project, says Premier Tony Wakeham.

items.title: Thousands of Alberta seniors to lose income supplement as province faces rising provincial deficit
items.summary_auto: Alberta is making cuts to seniors' benefits and tax credits for caregivers as it faces a $9.4-billion budget deficit.

items.title: With FIFA World Cup 100 days away, preparations continue in Toronto, Vancouver
items.summary_auto: Signs of the FIFA World Cup are slowly beginning to emerge in Vancouver and Toronto. Tuesday marks 100 days until the tournament kicks off with matches in Mexico City and Guadalajara, and as the games approach, organizers in both Canadian host cities are finalizing preparations.

items.title: Trump administration gives up legal battle to impose executive order on 4 law firms
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Justice Department has dropped a legal bid to revive President Donald Trump's executive orders targeting four prominent law firms over their past ‌legal work, diversity policies and political ties.

items.title: A timeline of how B.C. got to the decision to stop changing its clocks
N.S. farmers hope snowy winter will mitigate effects of drought
items.summary_auto: Most of the province has seen normal or above-average snowfall this winter, but there are a few factors that will determine how that affects the water table.

items.title: White nationalist fight clubs pose risk for 'extreme violence,' warns government report
items.summary_auto: The growing white nationalist "active club" movement poses a risk for "extreme violence" in Canada, according to an internal Public Safety Canada brief obtained by CBC News.

items.title: Why Trump still needs to sell Iran war to U.S. voters
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump has given Americans a short list of his objectives in attacking Iran, but when it comes to the justification for launching the war and how the conflict is expected to play out, he and his team are sending mixed messages.

items.title: An Ottawa parking garage partially collapsed a year ago and no one knows why. The owner plans to reopen
items.summary_auto: The owner of the downtown Ottawa parking garage where a large section came crashing down just over a year ago says it plans to rebuild and reopen the facility — though it remains unclear what caused the collapse, and the company is not sharing details about past inspections of the property.

items.title: Killer whale moms struggle to regain body fat with more offspring
items.summary_auto: New Canadian-led research into northern resident killer whales suggests even when food is plentiful, orca moms struggle to regain body fat when they have more living offspring.

items.title: Your teen is likely seeing 'looksmaxxing' content online. Here's what you need to know
items.summary_auto: Here’s what parents and other concerned adults need to know about looksmaxxing, and how to talk about it with youth.
